column5: with अग्रम् and gen.; cf. अग्र-णी), RV.

column7: Typo – This correction is a "Print Change". In this verse from the Mahabharata (Vanaparva / Book 3, Chapter 107, Verse 23), the sons of King Sagara (sagarātmajāḥ) are digging up the earth to find their sacrificial horse. The word bilaṃ means "hole", "cavity", or "chasm", which fits the action of digging (akhanan).
